enonce.texte:
📝 Exercise 1: Match the campaign and election vocabulary with their correct definitions.

Column A (Terms)

  • 1. Campaign
  • 2. Candidate
  • 3. Vote
  • 4. Prefect
  • 5. Election
  • 6. Manifesto

Column B (Definitions)

  • A. A person who runs for a position
  • B. The formal process of choosing someone for a position
  • C. A document stating a candidate's plans and promises
  • D. A student elected to lead others in school
  • E. A series of activities to get elected
  • F. The act of casting a ballot to choose a candidate

questions.texte: Match each term in Column A with its correct definition in Column B.

sousQuestions.texte: Campaign
sousQuestions.reponse: E. A series of activities to get elected

sousQuestions.texte: Candidate
sousQuestions.reponse: A. A person who runs for a position

sousQuestions.texte: Vote
sousQuestions.reponse: F. The act of casting a ballot to choose a candidate

sousQuestions.texte: Prefect
sousQuestions.reponse: D. A student elected to lead others in school

sousQuestions.texte: Election
sousQuestions.reponse: B. The formal process of choosing someone for a position

sousQuestions.texte: Manifesto
sousQuestions.reponse: C. A document stating a candidate's plans and promises

enonce.texte:
🔍 Exercise 2: Complete the sentences with the correct phrasal verb from the box.

Phrasal Verbs: stand for, vote for, run for, give up, look for, put forward, carry out

1. The students will _____________ the best candidate on Election Day.

2. The prefect candidates must _____________ their ideas during the campaign.

3. Many students want to _____________ the position of school prefect.

4. The candidate will _____________ his promises if he is elected.

5. She decided to _____________ her campaign despite the difficulties.

6. The students are _____________ a candidate who can represent them well.

7. What does the prefect candidate _____________?

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct phrasal verb.
questions.reponse: 1. vote for, 2. put forward, 3. run for, 4. carry out, 5. give up, 6. looking for, 7. stand for

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 1: The students will _____________ the best candidate on Election Day.
sousQuestions.reponse: vote for

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 2: The prefect candidates must _____________ their ideas during the campaign.
sousQuestions.reponse: put forward

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 3: Many students want to _____________ the position of school prefect.
sousQuestions.reponse: run for

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 4: The candidate will _____________ his promises if he is elected.
sousQuestions.reponse: carry out

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 5: She decided to _____________ her campaign despite the difficulties.
sousQuestions.reponse: give up

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 6: The students are _____________ a candidate who can represent them well.
sousQuestions.reponse: looking for

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 7: What does the prefect candidate _____________?
sousQuestions.reponse: stand for

enonce.texte:
📖 Exercise 3: Read the campaign speech below and answer the questions.

"Dear fellow students, teachers, and staff members. My name is Marie, and I am standing for the position of school prefect. I believe that our school needs a leader who listens to every student's voice. If you elect me, I will work hard to improve our school environment. I will organize activities that bring us together, like sports competitions and cultural events. I will also create a suggestion box where you can share your ideas. Together, we can make our school a better place for everyone. Remember, a vote for Marie is a vote for positive change!"
images: A student giving a speech in front of an audience at a school assembly, cheerful and confident, African school setting

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the campaign speech.

sousQuestions.texte: What position is Marie campaigning for? (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: Marie is campaigning for the position of school prefect.

sousQuestions.texte: List two things Marie promises to do if she is elected. (List)
sousQuestions.reponse: 1) Organize activities like sports competitions and cultural events. 2) Create a suggestion box for students to share ideas.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the meaning of 'A vote for Marie is a vote for positive change.' (Explain)
sousQuestions.reponse: This means that by voting for Marie, students are choosing to make the school better. She represents improvement, progress, and a better school environment.

sousQuestions.texte: Justify why Marie's speech is effective for a campaign. (Justify)
sousQuestions.reponse: Marie's speech is effective because it includes specific promises, addresses the audience directly, uses persuasive language, and shows she cares about students' opinions. It creates a sense of unity and hope.

enonce.texte:
✍️ Exercise 4: Write a campaign speech for a school prefect election.

Instructions:

  • Introduce yourself and state the position you are running for.
  • Mention two or three problems at school you want to solve.
  • Promise specific actions you will take.
  • Explain why students should vote for you.
  • End with a memorable slogan.
images: A student writing on a piece of paper with a thoughtful expression, at a school desk, African classroom setting

questions.texte: Write a complete campaign speech (150-200 words). Use phrasal verbs and persuasive language.
questions.reponse: Example speech: 'Dear fellow students and teachers. My name is John, and I am running for the position of school prefect. I believe our school faces several challenges that we must tackle together. First, the cleanliness of our school is a serious issue. I will organize weekly clean-up campaigns and encourage everyone to take responsibility. Second, I notice that many students are not involved in school activities. I will create more clubs and sports events to bring us together. If you vote for me, I promise to carry out all my plans and always listen to your suggestions. I will set up a suggestion box and meet with students regularly. I am looking for a school where everyone feels valued and happy. Together, we can make our school shine! Remember, a vote for John is a vote for a brighter future. Thank you!'

enonce.texte:
🎯 Exercise 5: Prepare a complete campaign package for a school prefect candidate.

Tasks:

  • Write a campaign slogan (5 words maximum)
  • Write a manifesto with 5 promises
  • Write a short dialogue between two students discussing the candidate
  • Create a simple campaign poster description
images: Students putting up campaign posters on a school wall, lively and engaging, African school corridor setting

questions.texte: Create all the campaign materials as requested. Each element should be original and creative.
questions.reponse:
Example:

1. Slogan: 'Your Voice, My Mission!'

2. Manifesto:
   a. I will improve school cleanliness.
   b. I will organize monthly sports competitions.
   c. I will create a peer-tutoring program.
   d. I will install a suggestion box.
   e. I will represent students' concerns to the administration.

3. Dialogue:
   Student A: 'Who are you voting for?'
   Student B: 'I'm voting for Marie.'
   Student A: 'Why?'
   Student B: 'Because she listens to everyone and has great ideas.'
   Student A: 'I agree. I like her plan for sports activities.'
   Student B: 'Yes, and she cares about the environment.'
   Student A: 'Her slogan says it all: "Your Voice, My Mission!"'
   Student B: 'Exactly! Let's support her!'

sousQuestions.texte: Campaign Slogan
sousQuestions.reponse: Your Voice, My Mission!

sousQuestions.texte: Manifesto (5 promises)
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Improve school cleanliness. 2. Organize monthly sports competitions. 3. Create a peer-tutoring program. 4. Install a suggestion box. 5. Represent students' concerns to the administration.

sousQuestions.texte: Dialogue between two students
sousQuestions.reponse: Student A: 'Who are you voting for?' Student B: 'I'm voting for Marie.' Student A: 'Why?' Student B: 'Because she listens to everyone and has great ideas.' Student A: 'I agree. I like her plan for sports activities.' Student B: 'Yes, and she cares about the environment.' Student A: 'Her slogan says it all: "Your Voice, My Mission!"' Student B: 'Exactly! Let's support her!'

sousQuestions.texte: Campaign Poster Description
sousQuestions.reponse: A poster with a photo of the candidate smiling. The candidate's name 'Marie' in big letters. The slogan 'Your Voice, My Mission!' in bold. A list of three key promises: clean school, more sports, student voice. A message: 'Vote for Marie!'
